svg.title: WhatsApp tick state machine — complete
svg.desc: Full tick state machine showing all five states: pending clock, single grey tick, double grey tick, blue ticks, plus the offline branch where single tick waits until recipient reconnects. Each transition is driven by an explicit ACK.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What does the clock icon mean in WhatsApp?')
sendPrompt('What does a single grey tick mean in WhatsApp?')
sendPrompt('What does the WhatsApp server ACK back to the sender?')
sendPrompt('What does the WhatsApp server do after it stores a message?')
sendPrompt('How does the recipient phone receive a WhatsApp message?')
sendPrompt('What is the difference between single and double grey tick in WhatsApp?')
sendPrompt('How does the server tell the sender that a message was delivered?')
sendPrompt('When exactly does WhatsApp fire a read receipt?')
sendPrompt('Can you disable blue ticks in WhatsApp and what is the trade-off?')
sendPrompt('How does WhatsApp handle read receipts when they are disabled?')
sendPrompt('How long does WhatsApp hold messages for offline users?')
sendPrompt('What happens to queued WhatsApp messages when you come back online?')

svg.text: Multi-device: server tracks tick per device
Sender sees most-advanced state across all linked devices
Opt-out: disabling read receipts also stops receiving them (WhatsApp enforces symmetry)
